title: Baton Slice Spec
description: The machine-readable contract for one slice: the user outcome, the in/out-of-scope boundary, the needs it covers, and its acceptance criteria as EARS-notation entries with trace links. Human-authored rationale lives in the free-text 'rationale' field; runtime state lives in status.json (slice-status-v1).

id_token.description: A clean identifier token. Disallows whitespace and ':' so a newline-fusion defect fails validation by construction.

ears_pattern.description: EARS pattern class. 'none' (free-form, unmatched) is deliberately excluded — it is a Rule 8 violation the lint catches, never a valid authored value.

path.description: Workspace-root-relative slash path. The engine applies the exact lexical, normalization, and confinement rules in baton/llm-checks/README.md before reading it.

release.description: Safe release-directory identifier. Required whenever references contains a contract or sibling-slice reference.

user_outcome.description: One sentence: the user-facing outcome this slice delivers.

rationale.description: Human-authored prose explaining the slice. Optional; never machine-parsed.

touchpoints.description: Files/paths this slice owns. Used by track-disjointness and design-fit checks.

references.description: Typed direct normative inputs consumed by spec-ambiguity. Engines scan only this field for referenced artefacts; prose, touchpoints, and test_refs are not reference-discovery surfaces. Optional for compatibility with pre-field specs; current planners emit it even when empty.

in_scope.description: The boundary of what this slice delivers — distinct from user_outcome (the one-sentence result) and from acceptance_criteria (the testable checks). Bullet-level statements of what's included. Empty array is valid only when the slice is trivial enough that user_outcome + acceptance_criteria already state the full boundary; the field must still be present (records-as-JSON: this was previously a `## In scope` spec.md heading, scraped rather than validated).

out_of_scope.description: Explicit exclusions — things adjacent to this slice that it deliberately does not do. Each item is a Rule 2 boundary statement, not a deferral (a deferral needs why/tracking/acknowledgement in status.json's open_deferrals; out_of_scope just draws the line so the implementer doesn't silently absorb adjacent work). Empty array is valid (nothing adjacent worth calling out), but the field must be present (records-as-JSON: this was previously a `## Out of scope` spec.md heading, scraped rather than validated).

covers_needs.description: Intake need ids this slice delivers (vertical trace up). Rule 8 requires every slice to thread to at least one need.

effort_complexity.description: Two-axis per-slice rating set by the planner during decomposition (ADR-0011 §3.7), each axis anchored to a named standard so low/high is decidable rather than a vibe. COMPLEXITY (Cynefin) drives model choice + verification rigor: low = Clear/Complicated (a knowable right-or-good answer exists; expertise, not discovery); high = Complex/Chaotic (the solution shape is emergent, or the blast radius is wide). EFFORT (relative agile sizing) drives timeout/retry budget: low = fits one implementer sitting on a bounded surface; high = multi-sitting or broad surface. The quadrant is DERIVED from the two axes and is only a human-readable consistency checksum — nothing branches on it; the two axes carry all behaviour.

quadrant.description: Derived checksum label: low/low=quick, high effort + low complexity=grind (known but voluminous), low effort + high complexity=puzzle (small but tricky), high/high=beast (big and hard).

risks.description: Risks the planner enumerated, each with its mitigation. A stated mitigation is binding direction for the implementer (the captain's design-review treats it as load-bearing, not advisory), not a suggestion.

mitigation.description: The concrete mitigation. Binding direction when present.

text.description: The acceptance criterion in EARS notation.

test_refs.description: Forward trace: test names/paths that verify this AC. Filled as tests land.
